Section  A:  Compensation  Caps  &  Stacking

value
 
per
 
driver,
 
per
 
calendar
 
month.
 2.  Credit  Stacking:  A  driver  may  not  receive  more  than  two  (2)  immediate  credits  
within
 
the
 
same
 
24-hour
 
window.
 
If
 
a
 
third
 
issue
 
occurs,
 
the
 
system

to
 
a
 
human
 
City
 
Manager.

and

tiers
 
only.

strategist_plan.reasoning: Gold-tier driver (24-month tenure, £12,500 LTV) reports 3rd airport short fare in 30 days at LHR T5: ~120 min queue wait, 1.5km trip. Meets SFP eligibility (wait >90 min, distance <3km). Prior plan was rejected for exceeding Gold SFP cap of £25; revised plan stays within cap and adds non-monetary queue relief plus escalation given the recurring pattern.
evidence_summary: Driver reported 2-hour queue wait and 1.5km trip at LHR T5
airport_short_fare_count_30d = 3, meeting recurring-issue threshold
Open ticket T-1001 (135 min wait, £6.20 fare) and closed ticket T-1007 (95 min wait, £8.50 fare) are prior SFP events at LHR
Driver is Gold tier with 24-month tenure and £12,500 LTV
SFP eligibility confirmed: wait 120 min > 90 min threshold; distance 1.5km < 3km threshold

proposed_actions.description: Standard Airport Short Fare Recovery credit applied to current LHR T5 trip
proposed_actions.reason: Maximum permitted SFP credit for Gold tier per Section B.1. £25 matches the Gold SFP cap exactly. INC-002 is reserved for multi-day systemic failures and not applicable here.

proposed_actions.description: One-time Airport Fast-Track Voucher for next calendar day at LHR
proposed_actions.reason: Non-monetary queue relief directly addressing the root pain point of long LHR T5 queues; zero monetary value so does not consume the £150 monthly cap or 2-credit/24h stacking allowance.

proposed_actions.description: Personal phone callback from City Ops within 24 hours
proposed_actions.reason: Gold-tier, 24-month-tenure, £12,500-LTV partner with a recurring pattern warrants personal acknowledgement; confirms credit, explains fast-track voucher, and informs of escalation.

proposed_actions.description: City Manager review of LHR T5 queue dispatch logic
proposed_actions.reason: Three short fares in 30 days at the same airport meets recurring-issue threshold for operational review. Operational/managerial action, not a driver-facing credit, so does not affect caps.

strategist_plan.manager_message: Hi Maria, thanks for calling in. We've applied a £25 Airport Short Fare Recovery credit to your account for today's LHR trip, and we'd like to send you a one-time Fast-Track voucher for tomorrow so you can skip the front of the LHR T5 queue. A member of our City Ops team will call you back within 24 hours, and we're escalating the LHR T5 queue logic for review given that this has happened to you multiple times. We appreciate your patience and your 24 months with us.
assumptions: Reported wait time of 120 min and 1.5km trip distance are accepted as given; no diagnostic contradiction in the provided profile or tickets.
T-1001 and T-1007 are treated as related instances of the same SFP pattern within the 30-day window.
The £25 SFP credit applies to the current incident only; no back-issuance for prior tickets in this plan.
User query referenced 'Maria D-456'; profile identifies Maria S. with driver_id D-LON-001; plan uses D-LON-001 as the authoritative identifier.
Escalation is treated as an operational action, not a driver-facing credit, and does not consume monthly or 24h credit allowances.
If a fourth short-fare incident occurs within 24h of this credit, Section A.2 requires escalation to a human City Manager and blocks further automated credits.

warnings: Unknown non-monetary action treated as warning: support_followup.
Unknown non-monetary action treated as warning: escalation.

result.final_answer:
Driver Retention Recommendation

Driver:
- Name: Maria S.
- Driver ID: D-LON-001
- Tier: Gold
- Risk level: high

Situation:
- Issue type: airport_short_fare
- Summary: Gold-tier driver (24-month tenure, £12,500 LTV) reports 3rd airport short fare in 30 days at LHR T5: ~120 min queue wait, 1.5km trip. Meets SFP eligibility (wait >90 min, distance <3km). Prior plan was rejected for exceeding Gold SFP cap of £25; revised plan stays within cap and adds non-monetary queue relief plus escalation given the recurring pattern.
- Evidence from support tickets:
  - Driver reported 2-hour queue wait and 1.5km trip at LHR T5
  - airport_short_fare_count_30d = 3, meeting recurring-issue threshold
  - Open ticket T-1001 (135 min wait, £6.20 fare) and closed ticket T-1007 (95 min wait, £8.50 fare) are prior SFP events at LHR
  - Driver is Gold tier with 24-month tenure and £12,500 LTV
  - SFP eligibility confirmed: wait 120 min > 90 min threshold; distance 1.5km < 3km threshold

Recommended Plan:
- short_fare_credit (25 GBP): Standard Airport Short Fare Recovery credit applied to current LHR T5 trip
- short_fare_credit (0 GBP): One-time Airport Fast-Track Voucher for next calendar day at LHR
- support_followup: Personal phone callback from City Ops within 24 hours
- escalation: City Manager review of LHR T5 queue dispatch logic
- Manager message: Hi Maria, thanks for calling in. We've applied a £25 Airport Short Fare Recovery credit to your account for today's LHR trip, and we'd like to send you a one-time Fast-Track voucher for tomorrow so you can skip the front of the LHR T5 queue. A member of our City Ops team will call you back within 24 hours, and we're escalating the LHR T5 queue logic for review given that this has happened to you multiple times. We appreciate your patience and your 24 months with us.
- Monitoring recommendation: Monitor driver activity and new support tickets for 7 days.

Compliance Validation:
- Status: approved
- Policy evidence: All monetary actions are within deterministic policy caps, and non-monetary actions are allowlisted or harmless.
- Rejected previous action: [{'action': 'short_fare_credit', 'proposed_amount': 30.0, 'allowed_amount': 25.0, 'reason': 'Proposed amount exceeds Airport Short Fare compensation cap.'}]
- Correction made: ['Reduce short_fare_credit to 25 GBP or below.']

Final Decision:
- Approved

data.reasoning: Maria S. (D-LON-001) is a Gold-tier driver with 24 months tenure and €12,500 lifetime value experiencing a recurring airport short-fare problem at London Heathrow. Three documented incidents in 30 days (T-1007, T-1001, and current call) with waits of 95-135 minutes producing fares under £10. This is a systemic queue-dispatch issue, not an isolated complaint, and requires premium recovery plus an operational intervention to prevent churn. Severity is HIGH given the recurring pattern, high-LTV Gold driver status, and material churn risk if unresolved. Root cause is likely LHR queue dispatch logic disproportionately assigning very short trips to long-waiting drivers. Retention lever is to combine meaningful monetary recovery (acknowledging the pattern) with an operational fix (queue bypass) addressing both the symptom and the cause.
evidence_summary: Profile D-LON-001: Gold tier, 24 months tenure, €12,500 LTV, 0 invalid complaints, 0.02 cancellation rate
Ticket T-1007: prior LHR short-fare incident, fare under £10 after long wait
Ticket T-1001: prior LHR short-fare incident, currently OPEN, fare under £10 after long wait
Current call: 1.5 km trip following long LHR wait, caller referenced as 'Maria D-456' (identifier mismatch but name/city/pattern consistent)
Sentiment: Frustrated
Driver status: ONLINE and reachable
No prior goodwill credits or compensation on record

proposed_actions.description: Premium Airport Short Fare Recovery for current incident
proposed_actions.reason: Recurring (3rd in 30 days) pattern warrants premium compensation over standard £25 INC-001

proposed_actions.description: High-Value Partner Goodwill credit
proposed_actions.reason: Gold, 24-month tenure, €12,500 LTV, zero invalid complaints; signals relationship valuation beyond the ticket

proposed_actions.description: Airport Fast-Track Voucher for tomorrow
proposed_actions.reason: Operational lever that bypasses the broken LHR queue once, directly removing wait exposure

proposed_actions.description: Escalate T-1001 and current incident to LHR queue-logic / airport ops team
proposed_actions.reason: Root-cause investigation needed with 5 business day SLA; addresses systemic dispatch issue

proposed_actions.description: Senior CS owner to call Maria back within 2 hours for personal acknowledgment and again within 48 hours with investigation status
proposed_actions.reason: Personal touch required for high-LTV frustrated Gold driver

proposed_actions.description: Defer growth quests Q-101 and Q-102 pending sentiment recovery
proposed_actions.reason: Avoid pushing more driving onto a frustrated driver

data.manager_message: Route this to a senior support manager for approval and personal callback within 2 hours. Maria is a Gold-tier, 24-month, €12,500 LTV driver experiencing a recurring LHR short-fare problem (3 documented incidents in 30 days). Recommend: (1) Issue INC-002 Premium Airport Short Fare Recovery £50 for the current incident, (2) Issue INC-007 High-Value Partner Goodwill £75 recognizing tenure and LTV, (3) Issue Q-103 Airport Fast-Track Voucher for tomorrow to bypass the broken queue, (4) Escalate T-1001 plus the new call to the LHR queue-logic/ops team with 5 business day SLA, (5) Assign senior CS owner for 2-hour acknowledgment call and 48-hour follow-up, (6) Defer Q-101 and Q-102 growth quests until sentiment recovers. Total monetary value £125. Expected outcome: churn risk reduced from High to Low and sentiment stabilized within 7 days pending ops follow-through.
assumptions: The caller's reference 'Maria D-456' refers to the same driver as profile D-LON-001 (Maria S.); driver identifiers in the user query do not match the profile ID, but name, city, and pattern are consistent
The 1.5 km trip mentioned on the call corresponds to the same LHR short-fare pattern documented in tickets T-1007 and T-1001 (e.g., £6.20 fare to a local Hilton)
No prior goodwill credits or compensation have been issued to this driver (no evidence in tickets or profile)
Driver remains ONLINE and reachable; sentiment is 'Frustrated' but not yet disengaged
LHR queue dispatch is managed by a separate ops/queue-logic team that can be escalated to

data.explanation: One or more actions violate deterministic policy caps.

data.reason: Critic rejected the previous plan. Routing back to Strategist for revision.

data.reasoning: Gold-tier driver Maria S. (D-LON-001) reports a recurring airport short fare incident: a 1.5 km trip after ~120 min wait at LHR Terminal 5, which is the 4th such incident in 30 days. Despite strong standing (24 months tenure, £12,500 LTV, 0.02 cancellation rate, 0 confirmed valid complaints), her sentiment is flagged as Frustrated, indicating elevated churn risk. Ticket T-1001 remains OPEN, compounding frustration. The pattern suggests a possible systemic queue logic issue, requiring both immediate in-policy recovery and City Manager escalation. The plan applies a cap-compliant £25 short fare credit (Gold cap per B.1, INC-001 not INC-002), issues a fast-track voucher to address queue pain, adds a conservative £30 Churn goodwill (Gold/Silver eligible per A.3), escalates to City Manager per A.2 for the recurring-issue pattern, and schedules a 48h callback. Total immediate credits £55, well under the £150 monthly cap.
evidence_summary: Trip T-2026-0317-005: 1.5 km distance after ~120 min wait at LHR Terminal 5 (under 3 km threshold, over 90 min wait threshold)
Ticket T-1007 (2026-02-28): prior short fare complaint citing 'something wrong with the queue logic'
Ticket T-1001 (2026-03-02): OPEN short fare ticket, unresolved
4 airport short fare incidents in trailing 30 days (3+ triggers City Manager escalation per A.2)
Driver profile: Gold tier, 24 months tenure, £12,500 LTV, 0.02 cancellation rate
0 confirmed valid complaints in trailing year; consistent narrative supports credibility
Recent sentiment: Frustrated

proposed_actions.description: Standard Airport Short Fare Recovery (INC-001) applied to driver account
proposed_actions.reason: Cap-compliant short fare recovery for Gold tier per Section B.1 (£25 max per instance). Wait time (~120 min) exceeds 90 min threshold and trip distance (1.5 km) is below 3 km threshold. INC-002 (£50) explicitly not used because this is a standard instance, not a confirmed multi-day systemic failure.

proposed_actions.description: One-time Airport Fast-Track Voucher (Q-103) valid for next airport shift
proposed_actions.reason: Gold-tier eligible non-monetary remedy that directly addresses the primary pain point: long airport queue waits. Zero monetary value keeps total well under monthly cap and provides immediate tangible relief.

proposed_actions.description: Churn goodwill credit for Gold-tier high-LTV driver with recurring issues
proposed_actions.reason: Retention gesture for high-LTV Gold driver (24 months tenure, £12,500 LTV) with Frustrated sentiment and 4 short fare incidents in 30 days. Churn goodwill credits are reserved for Gold and Silver tiers per Section A.3. Amount kept conservative at £30 to preserve monthly cap headroom and avoid overcompensation.

proposed_actions.description: High-priority escalation to City Manager - London to investigate queue logic and bundle open ticket T-1001
proposed_actions.reason: Driver has 4 short fare incidents in 30 days; Section A.2 mandates City Manager escalation for repeated issues. Driver explicitly cites 'something wrong with the queue logic' in T-1007. Open ticket T-1001 to be merged into the investigation outcome.

proposed_actions.description: Scheduled callback from City Manager within 48 hours to communicate investigation findings and confirm resolution
proposed_actions.reason: Follow-up touchpoint to deliver investigation outcome on queue logic review, close out open ticket T-1001, and confirm driver satisfaction after service recovery.

data.manager_message: Maria, thank you for calling and for your patience. We have applied a £25 short fare credit to your account immediately. We have also issued you a one-time airport fast-track voucher valid tomorrow so you skip to the front of the queue. Because this has happened to you repeatedly, we are escalating your case to our London City Manager today, and a team member will call you back within 48 hours with an update on the queue logic review and resolution of your open ticket.
assumptions: No more than one other immediate credit has been issued to D-LON-001 in the past 24 hours. This cannot be verified from the provided data; if two credits were already issued in the trailing 24h window, the third (this package) must defer to City Manager approval per Section A.2.

This incident is treated as a standard short fare recovery, not a confirmed multi-day systemic failure. The 4-incident pattern across T-1007, T-1001, and today's call is suggestive but not yet confirmed as systemic; City Manager escalation will determine whether INC-002 applies under systemic failure classification.
Driver D-456 referenced in the user query corresponds to D-LON-001 in the system profile (Maria S., London, Gold).

Confirmed_valid_complaints_1y = 0 means her claims have not been formally validated; however, low 0.02 cancellation rate and consistent ticket narrative support the credibility of her airport queue complaints.

data.manager_message: Hi Maria, thanks for calling in. We've applied a £25 Airport Short Fare Recovery credit to your account for today's LHR trip, and we'd like to send you a one-time Fast-Track voucher for tomorrow so you can skip the front of the LHR T5 queue. A member of our City Ops team will call you back within 24 hours, and we're escalating the LHR T5 queue logic for review given that this has happened to you multiple times. We appreciate your patience and your 24 months with us.
assumptions: Reported wait time of 120 min and 1.5km trip distance are accepted as given; no diagnostic contradiction in the provided profile or tickets.
T-1001 and T-1007 are treated as related instances of the same SFP pattern within the 30-day window.
The £25 SFP credit applies to the current incident only; no back-issuance for prior tickets in this plan.
User query referenced 'Maria D-456'; profile identifies Maria S. with driver_id D-LON-001; plan uses D-LON-001 as the authoritative identifier.
Escalation is treated as an operational action, not a driver-facing credit, and does not consume monthly or 24h credit allowances.
If a fourth short-fare incident occurs within 24h of this credit, Section A.2 requires escalation to a human City Manager and blocks further automated credits.

warnings: Unknown non-monetary action treated as warning: support_followup.
Unknown non-monetary action treated as warning: escalation.

policy_evidence.text:
FREENOW  Internal  Policy:  UK  Driver  Retention  &  Recovery  Guidelines  
Section  A:  Compensation  Caps  &  Stacking  
1.  Global  Monthly  Cap:  No  automated  retention  package  may  exceed  £150.00  in  total  
value
 
per
 
driver,
 
per
 
calendar
 
month.
 2.  Credit  Stacking:  A  driver  may  not  receive  more  than  two  (2)  immediate  credits  
within
 
the
 
same
 
24-hour
 
window.
 
If
 
a
 
third
 
issue
 
occurs,
 
the
 
system
 
must
 
escalate
 
to
 
a
 
human
 
City
 
Manager.
 3.  Tier  Multipliers:  Goodwill  credits  (Category:  Churn)  are  strictly  reserved  for  Gold  
and
 
Silver
 
tiers
 
only.
 
Section  B:  Specific  Issue  Resolutions  
1.  Airport  Short  Fares  (SFP):  *  Applicable  only  if  wait  time  >90  mins  and  trip  distance  
<3km.
 ○  GOLD  TIER  CAP:  £25.00  maximum  per  instance.  (Note:  The  AI  must  NOT  
use
 INC-002 (£50)  for  standard  short-fare  recovery;  that  is  reserved  for  
multi-day
 
systemic
 
failures).
 ○  SILVER/BRONZE  CAP:  £15.00  maximum  per  instance.  2.  Technical  &  GPS  Glitches:  *  Maximum  compensation  is  £10.00  per  confirmed  glitch  
(INC-003).  ○  Priority  Support  Access  (INC-004)  should  be  offered  for  recurring  geofence  
issues
 
(>2
 
tickets
 
in
 
7
 
days).
 3.  New  Starters  (Tenure  <3  Months):  ○  Priority  should  be  given  to  Commission  Shields  (INC-006)  rather  than  
direct
 
credits
 
to
 
encourage
 
continued
 
app
 
usage.
 4.  Infeasible  Quests:  ○  If  a  driver  completes  >80%  of  a  Quest  but  fails  due  to  documented  low  
demand
 
(diagnostics
 
confirm
 
<1.5
 
offers/hour),
 
a
 
Goodwill
 
Gesture
 
of
 
£20.00
 
is
 
permitted.

data.explanation: All monetary actions are within deterministic policy caps, and non-monetary actions are allowlisted or harmless.

data.final_answer:
Driver Retention Recommendation

Driver:
- Name: Maria S.
- Driver ID: D-LON-001
- Tier: Gold
- Risk level: high

Situation:
- Issue type: airport_short_fare
- Summary: Gold-tier driver (24-month tenure, £12,500 LTV) reports 3rd airport short fare in 30 days at LHR T5: ~120 min queue wait, 1.5km trip. Meets SFP eligibility (wait >90 min, distance <3km). Prior plan was rejected for exceeding Gold SFP cap of £25; revised plan stays within cap and adds non-monetary queue relief plus escalation given the recurring pattern.
- Evidence from support tickets:
  - Driver reported 2-hour queue wait and 1.5km trip at LHR T5
  - airport_short_fare_count_30d = 3, meeting recurring-issue threshold
  - Open ticket T-1001 (135 min wait, £6.20 fare) and closed ticket T-1007 (95 min wait, £8.50 fare) are prior SFP events at LHR
  - Driver is Gold tier with 24-month tenure and £12,500 LTV
  - SFP eligibility confirmed: wait 120 min > 90 min threshold; distance 1.5km < 3km threshold

Recommended Plan:
- short_fare_credit (25 GBP): Standard Airport Short Fare Recovery credit applied to current LHR T5 trip
- short_fare_credit (0 GBP): One-time Airport Fast-Track Voucher for next calendar day at LHR
- support_followup: Personal phone callback from City Ops within 24 hours
- escalation: City Manager review of LHR T5 queue dispatch logic
- Manager message: Hi Maria, thanks for calling in. We've applied a £25 Airport Short Fare Recovery credit to your account for today's LHR trip, and we'd like to send you a one-time Fast-Track voucher for tomorrow so you can skip the front of the LHR T5 queue. A member of our City Ops team will call you back within 24 hours, and we're escalating the LHR T5 queue logic for review given that this has happened to you multiple times. We appreciate your patience and your 24 months with us.
- Monitoring recommendation: Monitor driver activity and new support tickets for 7 days.

Compliance Validation:
- Status: approved
- Policy evidence: All monetary actions are within deterministic policy caps, and non-monetary actions are allowlisted or harmless.
- Rejected previous action: [{'action': 'short_fare_credit', 'proposed_amount': 30.0, 'allowed_amount': 25.0, 'reason': 'Proposed amount exceeds Airport Short Fare compensation cap.'}]
- Correction made: ['Reduce short_fare_credit to 25 GBP or below.']

Final Decision:
- Approved
